skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management using the inference.sh CLI platform
skill.summary: Post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, and follow users through the X API via inference.sh
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The external_commands patterns are markdown code fences (bash examples) not executable Ruby/shell backticks. The network URLs are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh. The cryptographic algorithm detection is a scanner error on YAML frontmatter and markdown table content. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code or security risks.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter/X posting and engagement workflows
content.value_statement: Social media managers waste hours manually posting and engaging on Twitter/X. This skill automates tweets, likes, retweets, DMs, and user management through the X API.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets and content with media attachments to Twitter/X
Like, retweet, and delete tweets programmatically
Send direct messages and follow users automatically
Retrieve tweet details and user profile information
Schedule posts for optimal engagement timing
Generate AI images and videos then post to Twitter
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Cannot post to multiple social platforms simultaneously
Dependent on X API rate limits and Twitter platform policies
Media posts require publicly accessible image or video URLs

use_cases.title: Content Scheduling for Social Media Managers
use_cases.description: Schedule and publish tweets at optimal times without manual intervention. Generate AI content and post automatically to maintain consistent social presence.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Automated Engagement Campaigns
use_cases.description: Like, retweet, and respond to relevant tweets based on keywords or hashtags. Follow targeted accounts to grow audience organically.

use_cases.title: AI-Generated Content Publishing
use_cases.description: Generate AI images or videos using integrated tools and automatically post them to Twitter with captions. Create full content workflows from generation to publication.

prompt_templates.title: Post a simple tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet with the text: 'Just launched our new feature! Check it out at example.com'

prompt_templates.title: Post with media attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a tweet with the text 'AI-generated sunset landscape' and attach the image from URL: https://example.com/sunset.jpg

prompt_templates.title: Engagement workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Find tweets containing #AI hashtag, like the top 5 results, retweet the best one, and follow the authors of tweets with over 100 likes

prompt_templates.title: AI content pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an AI image of 'futuristic city skyline at night', then post it to Twitter with caption 'AI vision of tomorrow's cities' and track engagement metrics

output_examples.output: Tweet posted successfully. Tweet ID: 1234567890. URL: https://twitter.com/user/status/1234567890
Tweet liked successfully. Author: @example. Like count: 1,542
Profile retrieved. Name: OpenAI. Followers: 2.1M. Bio: Creating safe AGI that benefits humanity.

best_practices: Authenticate with inference.sh CLI before running any Twitter automation commands
Test with sample inputs using --sample flag before executing production actions
Review Twitter/X API rate limits to avoid account restrictions
anti_patterns: Do not automate excessive posting or engagement that violates Twitter/X spam policies
Avoid posting identical content repeatedly across multiple accounts
Never automate interactions with sensitive topics like political campaigns or misinformation

faq.question: What do I need to get started with Twitter automation?
faq.answer: Install the inference.sh CLI using npm, authenticate with your Twitter/X account through inference.sh, then use the provided app commands like x/post-tweet or x/post-like.

faq.question: Can I post images and videos to Twitter with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use the x/post-create app to post tweets with media. Provide a publicly accessible URL to your image or video file in the media_url field.

faq.question: How do I schedule tweets for later posting?
faq.answer: The skill supports scheduling through the inference.sh platform. Add a scheduled_time parameter to your input JSON with an ISO 8601 timestamp.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with my personal Twitter account?
faq.answer: Yes, authentication goes through the official inference.sh platform which uses OAuth. Your credentials are not stored locally. Always review Twitter's automation rules.

faq.question: Can I combine this with AI image generation?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes workflows for generating AI images with tools like flux-dev-lora and posting them directly to Twitter in a single automation sequence.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Twitter's API rate limits?
faq.answer: The inference.sh platform will return rate limit errors. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff and respect Twitter's posting frequency guidelines.
